name: Docker Container Stats
description: Container-level metrics from a Docker host via the monctl-docker-stats sidecar. Captures CPU, memory, network I/O, and container state.
author: MonCTL
changelog: v1.0.0 - Bundles docker_stats 1.0.0 plus the docker_host device category, the Docker Host monitoring template, and the host_role label key.

apps.name: docker_stats
apps.description: Docker container resource monitoring via local monctl-docker-stats sidecar. Collects per-container CPU %, memory working set, network I/O, health status, and restart counts. Each container becomes a separate component (component_type=docker_container) in the performance table.

stats_url.description: HTTP endpoint of the monctl-docker-stats sidecar

versions.source_code:
"""Docker container stats monitoring app.

Polls the local monctl-docker-stats sidecar and reports per-container
resource metrics through the standard ingestion pipeline.

Config keys:
    stats_url   str  Sidecar URL (default: "http://localhost:9100/stats")
    timeout     int  HTTP timeout in seconds (default: 5)

Target table: performance
Each container becomes a separate component with component_type = "docker_container".
Host disk metrics are reported as component_type = "docker_host".

Metrics per container:
    cpu_pct            CPU usage percentage
    mem_usage_bytes    Memory working set (usage minus cache)
    mem_limit_bytes    Container memory limit
    mem_pct            Memory usage as percentage of limit
    net_rx_bytes       Total bytes received
    net_tx_bytes       Total bytes transmitted
    block_read_bytes   Total bytes read from disk
    block_write_bytes  Total bytes written to disk
    pids               Number of running processes
    running            1.0 if container is running, 0.0 otherwise
    health             1.0 = healthy, 0.0 = unhealthy, -1.0 = no healthcheck
    restart_count      Number of container restarts
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import time
import urllib.request

from monctl_collector.jobs.models import PollContext, PollResult
from monctl_collector.polling.base import BasePoller


class Poller(BasePoller):
    """Docker stats poller — queries local sidecar for container metrics."""

    async def poll(self, context: PollContext) -> PollResult:
        config = context.job.parameters or {}
        stats_url = config.get("stats_url", "http://localhost:9100/stats")
        timeout = int(config.get("timeout", 5))

        t0 = time.time()
        try:
            req = urllib.request.Request(stats_url)
            with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as resp:
                data = json.loads(resp.read())
        except Exception as exc:
            return PollResult(
                job_id=context.job.job_id,
                device_id=context.job.device_id,
                collector_node=context.node_id,
                timestamp=time.time(),
                metrics=[],
                config_data=None,
                status="error",
                reachable=False,
                error_message=f"Sidecar unreachable: {exc}",
                execution_time_ms=int((time.time() - t0) * 1000),
            )

        execution_time_ms = int((time.time() - t0) * 1000)

        # Build per-container metrics as separate components
        metrics = []
        for container in data.get("containers", []):
            name = container.get("name", "unknown")
            m = {
                "component": name,
                "component_type": "docker_container",
                "metric_names": [],
                "metric_values": [],
            }

            # Numeric resource metrics
            for key in [
                "cpu_pct", "mem_usage_bytes", "mem_limit_bytes",
                "mem_pct", "net_rx_bytes", "net_tx_bytes",
                "block_read_bytes", "block_write_bytes", "pids",
            ]:
                val = container.get(key)
                if val is not None:
                    m["metric_names"].append(key)
                    m["metric_values"].append(float(val))

            # Status as numeric (1 = running, 0 = not running)
            m["metric_names"].append("running")
            m["metric_values"].append(
                1.0 if container.get("status") == "running" else 0.0
            )

            # Health as numeric (1 = healthy, 0 = unhealthy, -1 = no healthcheck)
            health = container.get("health")
            health_val = (
                1.0 if health == "healthy"
                else 0.0 if health == "unhealthy"
                else -1.0
            )
            m["metric_names"].append("health")
            m["metric_values"].append(health_val)

            # Restart count
            m["metric_names"].append("restart_count")
            m["metric_values"].append(
                float(container.get("restart_count", 0))
            )

            metrics.append(m)

        # Host-level disk metrics (if sidecar reports them)
        host_info = data.get("host", {})
        if host_info and host_info.get("disk_total_bytes"):
            metrics.append({
                "component": "host_disk",
                "component_type": "docker_host",
                "metric_names": [
                    "disk_total_bytes",
                    "disk_used_bytes",
                    "disk_free_bytes",
                ],
                "metric_values": [
                    float(host_info.get("disk_total_bytes", 0)),
                    float(host_info.get("disk_used_bytes", 0)),
                    float(host_info.get("disk_free_bytes", 0)),
                ],
            })

        return PollResult(
            job_id=context.job.job_id,
            device_id=context.job.device_id,
            collector_node=context.node_id,
            timestamp=time.time(),
            metrics=metrics,
            config_data=None,
            status="ok",
            reachable=True,
            error_message=None,
            execution_time_ms=execution_time_ms,
        )

alert_definitions.name: Container high CPU (>80%)
alert_definitions.description: Fires when a container exceeds 80% CPU usage

alert_definitions.name: Container high memory (>90%)
alert_definitions.description: Fires when a container uses more than 90% of its memory limit

alert_definitions.name: Container restart loop
alert_definitions.description: Fires when a container has restarted more than 3 times

alert_definitions.name: Container stopped
alert_definitions.description: Fires when a Docker container stops running

alert_definitions.name: Container unhealthy
alert_definitions.description: Fires when a Docker container health check reports unhealthy

alert_definitions.name: Host disk >90%
alert_definitions.description: Fires when host disk usage exceeds 90%

device_categories.name: docker_host
device_categories.description: Server running Docker containers monitored by monctl-docker-stats sidecar

device_templates.name: Docker Host — Container Monitoring
device_templates.description: Monitors all Docker containers on a host via the monctl-docker-stats sidecar. Assign to a device representing the host, or directly to a collector with stats_url pointing to localhost:9100.

label_keys.description: Role of the host in the MonCTL infrastructure
